steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main idea of the passage. The decisive clue is the phrase "not yet commercially viable as a large-scale energy source," which signals that the passage is about what still needs to happen before the technology can be widely used.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "Continued research and development in artificial-leaf technology is needed before the devices can be widely used as an energy source.": This directly matches the passage's structure: the technology generates interest but is not yet viable, and scientists are researching improvements. ✓
  • Choice B — "The recent increase in the commercial use of artificial leaves as an energy source has encouraged many scientists to research.": The passage says the opposite — artificial leaves are not yet commercially used; the research is a response to that gap, not to increased commercial deployment. ✗
  • Choice C — "Artificial leaves split water molecules into oxygen and hydrogen gas using catalysts more efficiently than plants do using photosynthesis.": The passage never compares the efficiency of artificial leaves to natural photosynthesis; this is an unsupported detail. ✗
  • Choice D — "Artificial leaves were developed to mimic photosynthesis in order to store energy for long-term commercial use.": Mimicking photosynthesis is a background detail, not the main idea; the passage does not say long-term storage is the development goal. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
